analysis.name: Alexey Tumanov
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He seems to care a lot about the source material, but it was a struggle to follow. The projects are absurdly time consuming and the instructions were unclear. If a certain few TAs didn't exist this class would be impossible if you're new to the topic. Lots of information but I feel like I barely gained any knowledge due to the lack of clarity.

Terrible Professor and TAs, I did not enjoy the class, he was very unhelpful with matters such as labs and especially exams, my online exam refreshed 5 times but they blamed it on me, no help provided. The professors tone on emails seemed very unwelcoming and unfriendly. Labs were insanely long and expected effort was not correctly disclosed.

The professor was horrendous at teaching and not helpful whatsoever. The TAs provided limited help and didn't seem to care much, just like the professor. It could've been a good class but alas it wasn't. Please avoid this class, the course content is sub-par, the difficulty is insane and expectations are unrealistic.

Really interesting lectures, but I recommend this class be avoided if at all possible: the projects take absurd amounts of time. I typically spent in excess of 15 hours per week working on them. The project specs are always very unclear, and getting clarifications out of the course staff is time-consuming, often futile, and invariably frustrating.

Absolutely awful at teaching, and extremely unhelpful with clarifications on the homework's or the subject matter. None of the TA's knew what they were doing and made the already difficult class that much harder. The expectations in this class were impractical. And the professors attitude in an out of the class was overall negative.

analysis.name: Anqi Wu
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Too many terminologies make lecture hard to follow, many of them are not necessary to be introduced at very first. The definition of this course is ambiguous -- is it for experts or tyro? For experts, the course content is too out of date. For tyro, nana, it's not that friendly. Prof. Wu usually won't explain the foundation stuff. take again?No.
Boring lecture, total waste of time. The homework also makes no sense. No help before the exam. Make easy topics complicate and irritating.

Some homework and exam problems are worded so confusingly and different from what's taught in lectures that make you wonder if she reads her homework and exams before giving them out. Piazza questions can be left unanswered for days but when students complain, it's apparently our fault for not "not having an idea of the workload".

The lectures are way too technical and move way too fast, even for people who have a strong math and programming background. Homeworks are extremely confusing and the "extra credit" questions are not fair. Furthermore the grading policy is constantly being changed and updated without clear communication to the students.

Very disappointed after finishing this class, was meant to focus on Neural and Behavioral Data but Prof Wu essentially turned it into a core Machine Learning class, with unclear slides, tangential and contrived explanations, lack of appropriate expectations and shoddy homework assignments. I did fine on the class but it was a serious waste of time.

analysis.name: Alex Moutaftchieva
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great organization and structure throughout the course. Always accessible outside of class and clearly cares a lot about students' career placement. Consistently connects what you learn in class to real-world applications, which makes the material much more meaningful. Easily the best professor in the major.

Alex does a great job in teaching Intro to Construction Management. She introduces new concepts in a way that is digestible for all students. There were a lot of valuable lessons that came out of the semester-end project that could be applied to the Building Construction world.

Alex has a lot of industry experience and really cares about you paying attention in class. It's pretty obvious that she is teaching because she wants us to actually learn something, not just lecturing to lecture. Very open to questions and assigns work that is actually relevant to the industry and what we are learning.

analysis.name: Aibek Musaev
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 165 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: How can I begin to describe this class? Professor Musaev is hands-down one of the best professors I've ever laid my eyes on at Tech. I'm only 3 weeks into the course, but I've already learned so much about what I don't want to become in the future: a CS 2340 professor!

I honestly don't understand all the bad reviews. He is a nice professor and does a good job teaching concepts. Most students tend to just blame the professor whenever they struggle lol. Go to class, ask questions, and practice and you will be good. HWs are long and suck though.

Biggest troll I have encountered in my ed career. Copies slides from textbook & codes demos super fast. Instead of lecture read his slides, do slide practices & read textbook if stuck. TAs are amazing for HW help & explaining concepts. Start HW early. Teaches surface level of content to have time to ask riddles & gets mad if you know the answer.

What professor you take doesn't really matter in this course (idk if there's even multiple options). This guy seems alright though. The course itself is a decent amount of busy work and lecture attendance is required, but otherwise its chill.

This class has been the worst class I have ever taken. There were a million ways this class could have been structured better but the professor chose to make a project based class more dependent on rote learning 1200 slides instead of the project which you will spend most of your time on while being 27% of the grade. Some TA's also on a power trip

analysis.name: Caitlin Anderson
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Chill class. Not many assignments in her class and when you do have something due, she breaks it down to a tee what she wants making it really easy to do well. There's the group podcast project at the end which was fun to do and easy to do well in if you put in the effort. Try to take her if you can, you won't regret it.

Outstanding professor. Took class over the summer so was a bit rushed with only 5 weeks of material, but one assignment was due at end of every week (Friday). Two day extension always available. No final exam but English Department requires reflective ePortfolio. A bit tedious, but plenty of time is given in class to complete. Def recommend.

Very clear on everything needed to do well on any assignment, and was overall a fun class. Definetly one of the best English 2 professors; no horror stories (apart from in the novels we read/watch) that can be found from other classes.

In my class she claimed Frankenstein was the 'first transracial and transsexual person' so yeh she's a little woke, but who really cares? What matters is the grade you get, and with Anderson you are guaranteed an A. She has like 3 total assignments in the semester each of which won't take more than an hour and lets you scroll tiktok in class.
She made the class fun and the lectures were interesting. There were some assignments but they were all interesting to do and the group projects were fun.

analysis.name: Anant Paravastu
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He uses matlab very differently than 1371, so reference his example code and learn his style and adapt it to yours. He tries to be pretty accessible and cares about his students so put in the work and talk things out with him if you need help because he'll definitely tries hard to help his students.

Dr. Paravastu is very passionate, comes across nice and seems genuinely concerned, but he DOES NOT know how to teach 'Thermo'. He taught the class for the first time, only presenting derivations and concepts. But we are tested entirely on problem solving, with absolutely no practice given. His tests are nonsensical relative to what he teaches.

Dr. Paravastu doesn't fit in this class. He doesn't help develop a foundation, provides no relatable links to a student's previous knowledge, assumes the student follows all his disorganized derivations and messily structured notes, and makes his tests far harder than any examples or help provided. A worthless class that is unnecessarily hard.

I can't even fully blame him because the course is designed by the department in this way. Whoever thought thermodynamics is best learned through fill-in-the-blank notes should not be allowed to be designing courses. Professor is often not sure of what he is teaching as he is following someone else's lectures. ChBE should follow MSE and Dr. Speyer.

If possible, do not take him. He uses another professor's notes and it's often clear he isn't sure himself about what he's teaching us. I just couldn't understand how he wanted us to apply concepts we learned in class. Intentionally designs exams so nobody can finish and questions are worded weirdly too. But I can see he's trying.

analysis.name: Arnold Schneider
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 46 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Did not care about his students - wrote chicken scratch notes for every lecture that were based off accounting text books. Talks way too fast and writes so quickly/poorly, that it is just better to read the textbooks. Exams are extremely difficult and practice problems were not representative of biomedical engineering applications. Avoid Avoid!!!

Class time purely goes over examples from the book - don't expect to go into lectures and actually learn anything, the expectation is that class time clarifies what you've already learned. 

In class he reflects his hand-writing on screen and its awfully scratchy - it's incredibly hard to read and follow along. 

Terribly downgrade from Deby

Mr. Schneider knows accounting very well and ask lots of questions during class to get class participation, but I could never participate unless I read the chapters before hand.  Its all about working problems.  The material isn't hard but its not an easy A.  He isn't very eager to answer questions and can often be condescending.

Lectures somehow go too fast and too slow at the same time. He handwrites notes in class. Its supposed to line up with the textbook (that he wrote himself) but he jumps around like crazy and doesn't even cover everything in the chapter. Reading the textbook is equally difficult. Nothing is clear and practice problems don't have answers. Avoid avoid

This professor wasn't as bad as the reviews made him out to be. He's ok. lectures are kinda boring but he gives extra credit. Your final grade is based on tests and homework, there aren't any projects or anything like that. You get a cheat sheet on the tests. He grades pretty easy and gives partial credit where he can.

analysis.name: Abrahim Ladha
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 173 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Take if you are 100% sure that you will attend all lectures + solve all homework + all practice problems + spend a lot of time trying to combine your knowledge from the lectures with the extremely difficult practice problems, and expect even more "creative", harder questions to appear on your exam. You need to practice as if this is your only class

I think Ladha is a chill guy. However, it's pretty clear that the expectations of the course don't really align with the reality. I don't think the exams are fairly written and there should be, at the very least, an exam drop for this class (like other CS2050). The TAs need less power though; they practically run the course and not in a good way.

Why is this called an intro class. Homeworks take min 6 hrs a week, TAs cant help much, it's too hard. Test reviews are so useless from TAs. If you get a C on 1 exam, no chance of an A.

He's a nice guy, content itself isn't even really that bad, but the class structure is absolutely abysmal. Save yourself the headache and transfer in the credit.

Ladha himself is a good lecturer and a good person. It's his TA's that make the class a nightmare. Just because they went through hell in their semester they think it's their turn to give us hell back. HW's took 6+ hrs per week and exams had singular questions worth 5+% of your COURSE GRADE. Absolutely do not take this class under any circumstances

This class actively regressed my knowledge of discrete mathematics and math as a whole. If God wished to give his strongest warriors a challenge, he would send students to CS2050 Office Hours to face the awful TAs for this class. Such students would emerge Navy SEALs.  Ladha is a terrible lecturer and teaches content that cater to few. Do not take.

analysis.name: Andrea Parker
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The main grade for the class is a semester-long project, where you conduct user research and make Figma prototypes. It isn't bad if you have a good team, but it's a lot of writing and busy work. The lectures are honestly super boring and don't cover much to complete the project, so you don't need to pay attention to them.

I'm learning literally nothing in this class, it's the epitome of stupid busy work and wasting your time.
There's so much possible stuff to learn in the field of UI but she manages to avoid all of that useful stuff and find the most boring useless things to cover.

Great UX/UI course. I learned a lot and I found the class very useful since I want to do UX/UI as a career. The entire class is centered around group work however, so CHOOSE YOUR MEMBERS CAREFULLY. There is a lot of work, but they give you lots of time to work on it. Stay organized !!! Attendance done thru turning point/point solutions.

The class only consisted of attendance, small presentation, and semester long group project. The group project is the biggest part of the class worth 75% so choose your team members wisely. She had fun lectures to listen and learn more about UI/UX. Overall I enjoyed the class.

analysis.name: Rachel Behrmann
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 30 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I can't stress how easy this class with Professor Behrmann is. Attendance is technically mandatory but it's only enforced with participation quizzes that she gives every two lectures. I barely showed up but she uploads extensive notes, and allows you to have a flashcard for the exam. Weekly quizzes are open note and on Canvas, AND, no final.

Prof. Behrmann is awesome. She is super friendly and is extremely passionate about economics. The class is fast-paced and lecture heavy, but the exams are not that difficult. Assigns easy weekly HW quizzes that boost your grade and allows notecard crib sheet for every exam. Overall, I enjoyed taking this class and learned a ton.

Great professor with good lectures. The course itself is a bit challenging if you have never taken economics. The class is also very exam heavy (64% of grade) so slipping up once can mess you up for the whole semester. Overall not too hard or stressful though. Attendance taken only a few times using an access code.

Great professor. Super helpful and willing to answer any questions. You can always go to her office hours for help. Explanations are really good. Weekly quizzes and tests aren't too hard. Participation isn't required most of the time, but other times there are participation checks on canvas. Recommend taking her class!

As long as you show up and do your work, this class is very passable! Weekly homeworks that are not long and are very similar to the exams, plus you get a notecard for each exam. She is very accessible and wants you to do well in her class. Encourages coming to office hours and participating in class.

analysis.name: Justin Biddle
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Biddle is a very passionate and interesting lecturer, and the class is pretty discussion-heavy.

Dr. Biddle is NOT accessible outside of class. Does not respond to emails. This sucks because each absence from his class is 2% off final grade and it's hard to communicate. However, class is relatively easy and discussion based. I've learned a lot from his class. I just wish he was more lenient to absences and more responsive. He's cool I guess.

Dr. Biddle is a chill and awesome professor. I would recommend taking his philosophy class. Participation counts and the semester is broken into 3 parts: 1. do reading before class and have discussion on Perusall & have discussions in class, 2. individual essay (some in-class time given), and 3. group essay/presentation (in-class time given).

Dr. Biddle is a great professor and his lectures are interesting and enlightening. His class consists of daily readings and lectures that clarify the author's message. The only graded assignments are weekly pop quizzes to ensure you're actually reading, and take-home essay exams for the midterm/final. Understand the material and you'll do fine.

Prof. Biddle cares about his students, and wants his students to learn. Had to read before each class (1.5 hrs of work) to be able to do his pop quizzes and participate in class (there was no homework). However, pop quizzes are easy - they are very high level. The midterm and final were take-home, and consisted of a few essay questions.

analysis.name: Gerandy Brito
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 154 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Brito's teaching pace is great, and he keeps class very interesting: extremely funny. Sometimes doesn't follow the textbook or slides, but normally his ideas are better and simpler than those presented. Struggled to fall asleep in linear algebra, that's a feat.

I loved this class and Professor Brito. One complaint however is with the TAs. This is the first class where TAs are less approachable than the Professor and this might be due to how the class is structured. Some TAs were really nice and helpful but others TAs (namely one) on Piazza who would demean students constantly with their replies.

Brito is a great professor who clearly has a passion for the material and can keep students engaged. HW seemed like a lot for me but it really helped me learn the material. There is adequate study material for each exam and Brito tries to keep the class as engaging as possible.

Being an easy professor who gives high grades despite failing exams does not translate to being a good professor. He never released homework answers or exam answers or even feedback, just whether it was correct or not. This made doing homework worthless. For the final, he explicitly said specific topics were not on the exam--they were on the exam.

His tests are easier than the other sections, which is big in 1553. His lectures are pretty funny and entertaining, and he's a really nice guy. At first I thought he was going to be really disorganized, but he really knows his stuff and he's pretty good at teaching if you pay attention.

analysis.name: Rodrigo Borela
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 84 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Great professor. Interesting lectures. Maybe I'm just bad at coding, but for this class, you MUST lock in. Go to every lecture. Go to recitation. Office hours were so bad because they never had enough TAs for everyone. Sometimes I'd wait hours during office hours for a TA to help me.

I'm gonna preface by saying that I'm not good at CS, Rod was a decent teacher, he was quite passionate and funny and had pretty good lectures. For a GT requirement however, there was a lot of learning you had to do on your own that was not in the lectures, use the online textbook. Make sure you do the homeworks early and look at old exams.

Although the course material itself was difficult, the lectures were very thorough. Attendance not mandatory, but sitting through lectures really helped a lot on the tests. TAs were all very knowledgeable and he was very approachable if you had any questions. Tests can be difficult but past exams were very similar. Very sweet!

CS1301 isn't a real intro. Rod goes way too fast. Labs and TAs are awful. Never take robotics. They claim it's easier but it's hell, with async topics that don't belong in an intro. No lab help, TA helpdesk is slow and rude. Grading is sloppy, robots are disorganized. This class made me cry. Never again, made me reconsider my major as an EE.

I had literally no coding experience before going into class but found is really manageable. Lectures are recorded, so you don't have to go to class. I would recommend going to recitation and doing practice exams. Also there's like 2 points of extra credit added onto your grade at the end.

analysis.name: Matthieu Bloch
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 32 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I don't think he deserves all the hate that these reviews have been giving him. The class was fair and the exams were very similar to the homework. He is a nice person and mostly accommodating. He is very slow, but he also doesn't normally teach, and his section was only added to accommodate the influx of freshmen. This is not a deadly class.

Professor Bloch is an extremely caring professor, who is always willing to distribute knowledge on any subject he has on hand. Signal processing, machine learning, life lessons, he has and will teach about anything. Being in this VIP course has taught me so much, even when I started from no knowledge at all.

Lied to our faces about exam questions multiple times. Emphasized proof-based math questions instead of engineering/ system design ones, where the slightest error could cost you half credit. Only covered half of the material listed in the syllabus; we have to catch up now. His reviews for the graduate-level classes have the exact same complaints.

We got to early October's material in December. He regularly makes promises he doesn't keep.

He has described his own grading scheme as brutal. Every (lengthy) question is 2 points. If you write down anything you get 1 point. If you get the entire problem correct with no small errors at all, you get 2 points. 85% of the course grade was exams.

I thought he great, other guy talking about his french bit is just a hater. His lectures were super engaging and in depth, I feel like I really learned why things are implemented the way they are. The homework was also very helpful and translated well to the tests. It is true that he was behind in his material, but you can tell he's just passionate

analysis.name: Sven Behrens
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: PHYS 2231 (Honors Physics I) He's a super nice guy, not a super nice professor. I ended the class with a 79.4 and didn't get rounded up, but he rounded up another girl's 76 to a B. Tests were weighted pretty heavily and they were extremely difficult. Averages would be failing and he would say "I don't see any means for a curve"

Behrens is such a great professor and person. Took Phys2231 with him, and while it was an incredibly challenging class he worked hard to make sure every one of us succeeded. Put in the work to the class and he will put in the work to help you. Such a funny, earnest guy who really does care about his teaching. Got a 76 in class but rounded to a B.

He did a very good job of teaching the material and explained concepts well with good practice problems. Also is super funny and genuinely one of the most caring professors. Really wants his sections to succeeds, is super available outside of class, and frequently asks for and applies any feedback given very happily. Would definitely recommend.

I struggled significantly in this course, but that was no fault of his. Honestly, I believe I may not have even had the chance at passing this course without him. Dr. Behrens is very kind and considerate of his students. He goes above & beyond to help his students succeed. If you have to take this course, he is by far the best professor.
He was so kind and genuinely cared about each of us. He made sure his office hours accommodated every single student. He cares so much about us and physics which made the class more enjoyable.

analysis.name: Onur Birol
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 61 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Birol is one of the worst teachers I have ever had. He makes his tests overly difficult and is not open to the student's concerns. The test is in 50 minutes and I have to search through over 500 pages of notes so you're set up for failure. He doesn't respect students at all/very passive-aggressive. I would avoid him if you need to take BIOS 1108.

Made physiology very interesting and made topics relatable. Exams were straightforward, but some questions were difficult. Final exam was light, 60% of it came from previous midterms. You can do well by going to class or self-studying with the book. Homework = easy grade booster. Pre-lecture quizzes were annoying timing-wise, but he dropped a lot.

Dr. Birol co-taught this class with Dr. Richards. Birol is clearly passionate about biology. I went to 70% of the lectures and made an A. He is a good speaker and helps us understand material better. The only issue is that some of the exam questions were based on what was said in lecture and not found in the slides, but it wasn't that big of a deal

Dr. Birol's lectures were extremely helpful, and I felt that exams accurately reflected content taught in class. Pre-readings were required for this class. If I did not understand a pre-reading, I knew that if I went to class Dr. Birol would clarify it for me. Great teacher!!
To do well, attendance isn't necessary. Just rely on the textbook.

co-taught w/ robbie richards. birol focused on animals and was generally better. didn't ramble as much. he tries to draw focus to what goes on the test, but there's just a great amount of content. recitation is helpful. some tests focus on what is explicitly talked about in class and not clearly shown on slides, but attendance isn't mandatory.

analysis.name: Leonid Bunimovich

He interrupts you when your asking a question because he thinks he knows what your asking before your finished, so this process takes a while, by then you give up and learn nothing. Tests could be simple but not clear at what he wants you to give,  etc.

Horrible note-taking professor. He writes on the board in no particular order and then changes most of it later. Thick eastern-european accent, difficult to understand in lecture. To be fair, he was helpful when I had a problem with a test and have me a fair and deserved grade.

Appears very inviting of questions, but then calls them harsh names. 2/3 stopped attending after 1st lecture. >50% failed the first exam and the bell curve was a straight horizontal line. When approached, he got very close and said that "Other professors have a h€art atta¢k when they see me. I am the greatest statistician in the U.S."

He is horrible at explaining everything. There were topics I learned from other classes, but didn't understand how he did it. I asked questions about specific homework problems and he complained that "You're only asking questions about problems. You need to ask questions about concepts." Tests are NOTHING like homework. He's only at GT for research

The professor's teaching style is out of date. This should be an easy class and he makes it very hard by not being able to talk without 3-4 um's and uh's between 2 words. He is a very tough grader confirmed by course critique. The professor seems to be stuck in the USSR in mind. He might be very intelligent, but his skills in the classroom are weak

analysis.name: Bob Myers
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Clearly presents information and is a very good lecturer

I don't know why everyone is hating on this professor. His class is actually so funny. He is a little goofy and loves to talk about all things GT but his class is SO easy. Literally all you need to know is read the articles for the week and only write two reports the entire semester. This class is so easy and he's so funny.

Has to be the worst professor I had at Georgia Tech. He isnt a very nice person and has very little understanding of what goes on in students lives. His lectures are ultra boring as he goes on the incoherent ramblings that are just weird and awkward for the whole class. He assigns a case study every night for homework and his tests are so unfair!

Kind of a waste of time and money. He doesnt really teach anything practical in this MGT3510 class. He essentially just goes over a lot of case studies as you have one every night for homework. The also gives you a blank notes packet to fill out in which he hardly covers any of the notes. Then on test days he asks super specific case questions

Hands down worst professor I have ever had at Georgia Tech, the professor goes on and on about random topics in class. In addition he has side conversations with individual students so it seems like he ignores half the class and it makes it super awkward. Definitely take another professors supply chain/IT elective if you can!

analysis.name: Calton PU
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 30 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Pu is the most inconsiderate and laziest professor at tech In lecture we watch videos of him softly mumbling slides in a thick accent 2-4 papers due weekly The week where our semester group project proposal was due we had 4 papers due An extension was requested He responded by blaming students whom he called noise makers for gaming his class

He asked us to summarize each lecture in which he only played pre-recorded videos reading each word on the slides out. Two reading assignments on each week requiring one-page commentaries. He was extremely MEAN when commenting on project proposals, showing his perfect mastery of rhetorical questions and sarcasm. DON'T TAKE THIS COURSE!

NEVER TAKE THIS COURSE. Firstly, he is very aggressive and inconsiderate, and any reasonable request is called gaming his course. Secondly, most self-drafted semester-long projects are said to be worthless, and he expects us to produce more creative and better projects than commercial products. Do not let this course be your nightmare.

Despite being somewhat easy, this was by far the WORST class I have ever taken. He just played videos of himself talking during class, which were incredibly boring, unclear, and unnecessary. The requirements for the class are unclear, which he justifies by claiming that its about "autonomous learning." Poorly structured and poorly taught. Beware.

Replays video lectures during in-person class times. Will repeatedly interrupt you during office hours. Dislikes being questioned on the structure of the class to the point of sending long rant-like emails not even a month into the semester. Incredibly time consuming class with most of the difficulty coming from the interactions with the professor.

analysis.name: Charles Peak
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: unorganized + needs a writing class since he's bad at writing coherently and without grammatical/phrasal errors making it harder to answer basic questions. When questions arise during exams, he wastes precious time by simply reading the question again. I also have eyes. That is unhelpful.

He is approachable one-on-one outside of class, but grading is very strict and can feel snarky. Assignments often lack clear guidance, yet points are deducted for minor errors like spelling. The class may feel discouraging, but students can improve by adapting to his expectations.
Snarky and rude. Picky comments and feedback on work. Could/should have shown more kindness towards students. Don't take his class if you're looking for respect.

analysis.name: Colin Parker
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 46 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: He expects you to have come into class having a background in Physics and also keeping up with the textbook readings. (note: textbook is paid). In my opinion, I thought he focused too much on ideas that didn't matter that much to the class like derivations, and wouldn't spend enough time on the bigger picture that would be important for exams.

The class is structured well, especially for a class that most of the school takes. Dr. Parker seems very knowledgeable, but often expects us to know the concepts before lecture, and spends more of lecture doing examples when a lot of us don't know the concepts. Physics at Tech is hard, and I ended up watching Dr. Munoz's lectures to study.

I took AP Physics C1 in high school, so I may be a bit biased, but I thought Parker was a pretty good lecturer. I took AP Physics C in hs and without that I would've been cooked. He often spent most of the lectures trying to figure out practice problems for himself and would get them wrong everyday. It really annoyed me that someone with a physics PHD couldn't even teach physics 1 well. Just watch Alicea's lectures and ignore him.

definitely not intro friendly so if you haven't taken physics before, don't take this professor. its easy to get lost in lectures and i would often find myself more confused after. physics is just hard in general so take it easy on yourself and take alicea if possible

analysis.name: Carla Gerona
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Truly horrible class. She struggles with technology, gives heavy readings weekly with around 4-5 hours of lecture time per week. Everything you do will contain a quiz. You will have to memorize EVERY detail of the textbook if you want even a semi-decent grade. Many trick questions on the test asking information not in the textbook or class. AVOID.

This class was harder than every one of my major related classes. Get ready to spend about 15 hours per week on this class, and then barely passing because you didn't remember what color George Washington's uncle's underwear was at the battle of peepeepoopoo.

My relationship with this class was more toxic than my relationship with my ex. I put in around 16 hours of work per week, do all the assignments and readings, and I prepare relentlessly for exams. There is nothing I could have conceivably done to memorize the sheer level of detail that her tests require, if you want to even PASS. AVOID AT ALL COST

Prof. Gerona is wonderful. Her online lectures are engaging and a good balance of independence. Just make sure you do not forget about the deadlines or you will need a deans letter, no exceptions. Exams are very hard, so make sure you rigorously review the lectures-- they have all the info you will need! The lectures are interesting tho, really.

If you like reading through 905 pages of mostly unimportant textbook information from 1500-1877, completing quizzes that have little to no connection to the class and don't give an accurate representation of the material, and having to memorize how many pounds of meat and canoes two guys ate and built in 1837, this is the class for you.

analysis.name: Thomas Collins

review_highlights.comment: There were 2 projects with opportunities for extra credit if you put in significantly  more work. Imo project 2 way easier than project 1. 2 projects with plenty of extra credit. All lecture slides are online. Make sure to start projects and homework early. Plenty of practice tests available on canvas. Office hours help a lot from what I have heard. Great professor, but I have heard Wills might be slightly better, but both are great. Pretty easy class, not very test heavy.

Pretty good professor, makes it very clear whats going to be on the exams. the 2 projects are weighted the same as the 3 midterms, so gotta start those projects early. Lots of opportunity for extra credit with the projects, and very lenient late policy.

A really enjoyable and light class. Exams are extremely fair and there is ample opportunity to get extra credit on the two projects. Though, both of them are pretty time consuming. Highly recommended

analysis.name: Carrie Shepler

review_highlights.comment: I love Dr. Shepler! Her lectures are easy to follow and she makes a lot of difficult concepts easy to understand. She will also go out of her way to provide office hours and extra help to those who need it. That being said, this class requires a lot of work, especially with the lab component added on. You have to put in a lot of effort to get an A.

Dr. Shepler is awesome. I had her for 1211, and I have her now for 1212. She is always willing to help you and is very supportive both academically and personally. a professor that does the flipped classroom model correctly. office hours are good, lectures are good, and videos are good. I would recommend using the videos for cheat sheets because if it's in the vids it'll be on the test (lectures get derailed sometimes). Make sure to get all the final exam tokens. I love this professor!!! Please take her if you want amazing lectures, a kind and caring teacher, and just a great experience with CHEM1212K :) Dr. Shepler puts her lectures online in a flip classroom method but the videos are a masterpiece. They are super easy to follow and she explains everything in a clear and consice way. She is just wonderful.

Dr. Shepler is easily one of the most empathetic and sweet professors to roam this Earth. She structures the class with the understanding that this course is difficult. I won't lie and say the class is easy, but she definitely does everything in her power to ease the burden on her students. If you have the choice, don't take 1212 with anyone else!

I absolutely loved her class - she is engaging, kind, and interesting. The class is not difficult. Show up, participate, and do your work to earn an easy A!

Professor Jankowski is a great professor. He was a good presenter and would explain in class. It is easy to access him via Piazza, office hours, or after class. really wants you to get better at the language so lots of speaking involved. theres a good deal of work to do outside of class but not too much or difficult to deal with.

it takes weeks for grades to be returned to you. in addition, the criteria for the lengthy reflection portion of projects is extremely unclear, and the weight for each project in canvas is wrong, causing unnecessary stress for students. lectures were also incredibly hard to follow. don't take this class.

Dr. Sokol is a great teacher and very skilled at summarizing things into their core substance and imparting the knowledge. I did not realize how great Dr Sokol was until I took a few more professors to compare. He's really been the best.

analysis.name: Kevin Johnson
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 97 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Class structure is horrible, the pre lab quizzes somehow are worth more than the actual labs. The quizzes focus on minute details of the pre lab and actually don't test your knowledge on Digital Design concepts at all! The labs were actually quite fun and engaging and the TA's are awesome. Kevin himself is sort of just there. Final is horrible.
it seems like kevin puts all of his teaching effort into making this course less fun.

This class is called Digital Design Lab yet the majority of the assignments are about semantics. Kevin treats Piazza as a way to give students a scavenger hunt instead of just simply answering questions brought from the confusion of his own instructions. Also assignments seem to have random due times, sometimes 11:59 pm sometimes 6:30 pm, or 8:00.

The test and the quizzes were based on how well you can remember miniscule details instead of actual comprehension. Kevin himself is very narcisitic and unhelpful when you ask any type of question. Dont even try asking a question on piazaa, as he will simply answer it with another question making you even more confused.

If you can take another professor for 2020 do it. Kevin's lectures are overly dry and simple especially compared to homeworks and exams. His grading criteria is very unclear and he'll take off a lot of points on tests with no clear reason. Somewhat condescending when you ask questions. TA was great though.

analysis.name: Shana Kerr
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 62 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Seems really approachable in class BUT BEWARE if you go to her office. You will likely see students in tears.  She is not known for her personality!

Kerr seems approachable and nice when you first start out, but it is quite the opposite! She talks faster in her lectures than any human should ever be able to speak. Be prepared to "infer" (her words) what parts of the book you should be reading because the readings don't match the homework or the lectures and of course not the test.

The most important thing to do is to read and take notes on the textbook, as her lectures are basically regurgitating that. You can get around the participation by doing the learning catalytics from home; going to the lecture isn't necessary as long as you read the textbook. The tests can be difficult, but reading the textbook helps.

She goes so fast you cannot possibly take notes, and doesn't explain things well. She doesnt take time on the parts that are the most confusing, and goes on long tangents about stuff that isnt relevant. There are assignments unrelated to the content are harshly graded, and take up time. During office hours she is condescending and confusing.

I saw the other rating, and it's accurate. She appears nice and approachable but when contacted one-on-one does not care about the success or feelings of students. Also, her lectures are a little all over the place because she often loses her train of thought and often can't answer questions confidently.

analysis.name: Vladimir Kolesnikov
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The professor genuinely seems like a cool guy who is very knowledgeable, but he's condescending if you don't understand advanced concepts he touts as basic. Quizzes impossible to prepare for, no matter how much you study. He teaches concepts in class + textbook is informative, but the quizzes test situational what-ifs and suck and are unfair.

While his slides are clear and he communicates the material somewhat well, his quizzes and exams are obtuse and impossible to prepare for. I understand the material. This material makes sense. However, every quiz and exam is a roll of the dice.

Prof Vlad is a decent professor. First half of the class was very theory heavy. Second half was more projects and assignments. Weekly quiz and they were difficult, which would be open for the first 10 mins of class. One midterm which was worth 30% of your grade. It's not an easy class you can breeze thru.

Took blockchain section with Vlad. Fantastic dry nerd humor. The theory lectures (1st half) are hard but Vlad does a great job explaining. Has the audacity to ask the class if a quiz was easy when the median is 60. Projects, both research and programming, are fun and good additions. Not hard to get an A if you try

analysis.name: Lauren Steimle
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Best professor I've had at GT. Lectures are thorough and make concepts easy to grasp if you put in the work. Homework is hard but great exam prep. Exams are heavily weighted; midterms were easy, final was difficult (I suspect they apply a curve - they dont give us the final back). Group simulations were boring and time-consuming with random groups

Dr. Steimle is one of the best professors at Georgia Tech. Her lectures are exceptional and she provides plenty of help resources as well as very interactive office hours. The class itself wasn't hard at all, but you just need to make sure you don't get lost and she makes sure to put enough resources for that to be totally on you. Take her class!!!

I have never enjoyed a class as much as I enjoyed this one. Her class was incredibly enjoyable and the weekly study sessions she hosted were beneficial. If you have the opportunity to take this class, you should hope she is your professor.
Spectacular professor, probably the strongest I have had so far. Dr. Steimle has a lot of passion and breaks down difficult concepts clearly.

Dr. Steimle's 3232 class is one of my favorite classes I've taken at Tech. She clearly cares a lot about the content of the course, and she literally gets a smile on her face whenever students ask a question. Really great lectures, building up concepts from the ground level so that the intuition behind everything covered is very clear.

analysis.name: Linda Wills
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 45 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Wills is a great professor and I would highly recommend her for ECE 2035. I was teetering between taking 2035 or 2036 bc I'm EE, but I chose to take 2035 because I heard she is a great professor and that was definitely true. Clear lectures and expectations. Just make sure to pay attention in class and you will be ok!

Honestly if you spend a good amount of time on the projects, you should be set for the class. I got a 50 on the 1st project and still ended with an A after doing extra credit on proj 2. Exams are fine as long as you do the past exams she gives out! Pretty fun game project.

Very easy class, exams are very easy as long as you practice. Start on the projects way ahead of time. Lots of opportunities to get extra credit and good grades. Weak lecturer in my opinion, but not too bad. Overall, class was very fun and the content was very interesting to learn

Prof Wills was great for 2035. Sometimes she can be a little scattered during lectures but she is very intelligent and knows what she is doing. If you don't understand something in class she is very approachable during office hours and will help you. The projects can be difficult, but some involve extra credit. Tests can be tricky. Study study.

Don't even scroll any further down the page! JUST TAKE HER!!! Tips: - Start the final project as soon as you get the PDF. I started the final project the week before thinking I would have enough time but was not able to finish - Don't undermine the extra credit - Practice exams are golden resources for the exams. Do as much of them as possible

analysis.name: Mike Lehman
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 34 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Unnecessary, repetitive workload; Delayed feedback + grading; Unclear directions; Boring lectures; Says he's available outside of class if needed; Bulk of grade dependent on group work; Kind of goofy; If you submit + try on everything, you'll probably get an A, but he'll make you sweat for it

Assignments are typical for an English class, but many of the instructions for larger projects can be too vague and "open ended" leading to confusion and lower grades. Feedback is not good and inaccurate, and when asked about it he can be very condescending and counterproductive. Depends on his mood sometimes but got an A so its not impossible

assignments are pretty lengthy but have extremely ambiguous grading criteria, overall chill but tedious class. However, talking to him was like talking to a brick wall. impossible to get clear feedback and he regurgitates buzz words in a circle. Tanked a 60 because I was so fed up trying to talk to him. Not the worst but heavily recommend diff prof

not a hard class but def my least favorite english class I've ever taken. no assignment was difficult, but most things he would grade for completion until he randomly gave an 80 on an assignment worth 20% of your grade. I think pretty much everyone gets an a since he is generous with participation and portfolio grades.

I would recommend Dr. Lehman. I had him for thesis writing, and he provided good feedback to help get it polished before submission to my advisors. Was also really flexible with deadlines if you email before the due date.

analysis.name: Ling Liu
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 30 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I have been in GT CS for 5 years and this is THE worst of all the ones I have taken. The instructor is knowledgeable about the content she teaches. However, she is extremely rude to students and makes the course overly arbitrary. The rubrics for assignments are vague and the workload is too heavy for the final paper. Time wasted.

The CS6220 course experience is one of the worst at GT CS. TAs are non-existent. Homework is poorly designed with little instruction on what to do. Grading is quite random as well with no clear standard. However, if you just need an easy A and don't care about course experience, this course might actually be pretty nice.
Using public humiliation as a form of motivation is not valid. I don't need to know that another student is doing poorly in the class or is going through "family issues"

This is not a Systems course. This is rather a collection of topics the instructors is excited about. The same is reflected in the assignments, project-topics, and the review subjects. This class felt more ML and LLMs heavy than Systems heavy.

Also, lectures felt very vague, and most of the learning happened during the assignments and projects.

Genuinely a crashout, rude, grades hard for the projects and has a huge final paper. Haven't finished yet, but I've heard the curve is huge because of this. Most of this wouldn't be too bad if she wasn't extremely rude too.

analysis.name: Chin-Hui Lee
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: His lectures have to be the worst I've ever experienced. This course is on the harder side, but Dr. Lee can't explain a single concept clearly; all of his lectures feel like a review session, and he teaches like you already know everything about the topics. Might as well read the whole textbook. He cares about students, but absolutely can't teach.

I just took his class and got a C average. I could have done better by working harder to understand the course, but I might as well not have even attended the lectures. His teaching style really doesn't communicate concepts or expectations well. You'll succeed best if you treat it like a self-study. Overall, I would recommend exploring other profs.

Your succes in this class depends on who your professor and recitation prof is. Prof Lee seems to be a nice guy but can't explain a darn thing!  Even when asked a direct question to clarify a topic he was not able to give a direct answer. If you are the type of learner that needs to understand what is actually happening behind the scenes its a NO!

You will read the entire text book in this class cover to cover. The labs are time consuming but not difficult with a solid understanding of matlab. His lectures are not very exciting, he is somewhat monotoned. However, he is very accomplished an offers a wealth of knowledge. This course is the perfect example where practice makes perfect.

The class is known to be difficult and requires a lot of work, so it may not be entirely his fault. There were homework and labs every week, often on topics we would cover after homework was due. The homework was extremely helpful for tests, and the class was curved in the end. Lecture seemed to gloss over topics.

analysis.name: Chao Li
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: 学习中文很有意思！李老师 has been my favorite professor at Tech so far. I came into the class knowing no Chinese but feel much more confident now. Homework can be a lot (Chinese is hard) but is well worth in my opinion.

Literally one of the goats of all time. Li laoshi is really kind and understanding and tries his best to make Chinese as easy as possible for us (it is a hard language). Quizzes and tests are pretty easy if you do your hw and study. He's very silly and also gave us chinese snacks twice :))

Awesome professor, very kind and caring towards students. Although Chinese in general is a pretty difficult language, Li Lao Shi will help you a lot and is extremely lenient in grading as he clearly wishes for all of his students to be successful. Take his class, you won't regret it!
I learned so much Chinese from him. When I started the class I didn't understand a single word. Coming out of the class I am basically fluent in speaking now.

Li Laoshi is one of the nicest teachers at tech (He bought us mooncakes so we wouldn't stress for our first quiz). He understands that chinese is hard, and that we have other classes: work load is very manageable, and he will help students who are struggling, even on dictations and quizzes. I am not a native speaker, but I have learned much from Li

analysis.name: Sasha McBurse
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: Poor lecturer; goes deep on the wrong content and gives actively problematic assignments. Take her class if you're okay with location-verified attendance, taking photos of all your meals, and writing research papers where 16 peer-reviewed journal articles receives half marks on the rubric. TL;DR - You'll get an A, but at what emotional cost?

Although lectures were mandatory, we all just did work for other classes/ played Clash etc. She knows we all don't take this class extremely seriously, so she makes her lectures very fun and social. Had more fun in this class than I expected. She kept lectures engaging, and provided more than enough extra credit opportunities so there is no reason to not get an A. Just complete the assignments/hw on time and put a decent amount of effort into the final project and you will be fine. Mostly did other work during class.

Just show up to class and do other work. As long as you set aside 1.5 hours for each homework assignment, 3 hours for project, and do decent on the take-home quizzes, your grade in this class should be a fairly easy A. My friends who took online lecture said their class was easier, still an easy A either way, however.

She is an obstacle to one's education. Not because the class is in any way difficult, but because that is all the class serves as. She could very well cut the filler and give students lectures and quizzes every so often, instead she decides to be a nuisance and treat her students like a kindergarten class.

analysis.name: Martin Mourigal
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Mourigal is a great professor, he was excellent at teaching, fun, and he really cares about the success of his students. The difficulty of the class is due to the physics department, not to him personally, and he really tries to help you learn the material. Overall, he's a great professor and my favorite I've had after a year at Tech.

Prof. Mourigal is a great lecturer, he clearly is very interested in the subject matter and engages his students. That being said, classical Physics 2 is one of the most difficult classes I've ever taken. Expect to work in this class.

He is excellent at explaining what is one of the hardest subjects at Tech. Had the best lecture notes I've ever gotten from a teacher and he was pretty good about posting them after each class. Those notes were a life saver during homework and while studying. Keeps class interesting and is pretty funny

Mourigal cracks jokes and is funny. Lectures: he writes everything down on a whiteboard (no slides), so pay attention to his drawings/key concepts when he writes them. He doesn't cover new material super efficiently during lecture, but the next day, he'll do "review" of the previous lecture, clears up confusion. Expect exams to be pretty difficult.

Physics is a slug at GaTech because they make you do a lot of work and the coordinator makes the tests really hard. Through all this though Martin is a great teacher! Hes funny and engaging, if there is one thing I wish he used slides for notes instead of just using the board as it is hard to follow later. That nitpick though, Martin is the goat.

analysis.name: Michael Specter
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently

Very knowledgeable professor with highly-relevant industry experience with Operating Systems and security. Attendance is mandatory due to pop-quizzes. Projects are difficult... but it's a difficult course in general. Lectures are helpful in theoretical understanding. Projects test applied understanding.

This guy is the least accessible professor I have ever taken a class from. It feels bizarre to be in a class of less than 20 people and still somehow never be able to get in to contact with the professor. If you ever attempt to approach him after class he will instruct you to just email him. If you ever attempt to email him he will not respond.
Lots of reading, but interesting work and research!

analysis.name: Maysam Nezafati

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Nezafati is a GOAT! He is a very understanding and caring professor who takes time to make sure his students understand the concepts of class, not just a pattern for problems. PSS was very helpful, and his lectures reinforced material well. The homework was time consuming and quizzes were hard, but by the exams you were good and an 83 was an A.

Dr. Nezafati cares a lot about his students and if you're going to take bmed2110 definitely take it with him. The class itself is a lot of work but if you take the time to watch the lecture videos and understand the homework, the quizzes and tests aren't terrible.

Great teacher! 3 exams (1 was the final) - each one was based on what we had learned since the previous exam so nothing cumulative and each one had 3 attempts (with grades averaged). Explained everything very well. Rounded my high B up to an A (I went to every class and did every assignment) and I'm very thankful. Very manageable class!

Dr. Nezafati has been a great 2110 professor, especially with learning such different concepts than normal classes. He has always been very open to questions and helping outside of class. He seems to really enjoy the material and makes the class a lot more interesting. There is a lot of homework but TA hours are always really helpful (PAUL!!!)

Dr. Nezafati was very enthusiastic about the material. He explained everything very well and was very helpful when asked questions. He emphasized the small details, which are very important for this difficult course. He also made the online lectures this semester which were great. Definitely wouldn't want to take this class with anyone else.

analysis.name: Nivedita Bhattacharya
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Bhattacharya is an amazing teacher and her class notes are the best! She provides a lot of learning resources and explains concepts very clearly. But what stands out most of all in my opinion is her respect for the students.

She was a true gem virtually. Whenever I needed some clearing up or had a question on piazza, she answered in LESS THAN AN HOUR. Her lecture videos were very well done. Even when I had technical issues on the first exam, she let me email my exam to her with no complications, she was totally understanding. I WISH I COULD TAKE HER AGAIN!

Professor Bhattacharya was very knowledgeable and delivered clear and informative lectures.
She doesn't go too fast, and you can tell she cares about her students. Also answers questions thoroughly.
Very clear on how to succeed in the class, and she provides numerous resources like class notes and practice
exams to help you.

Professor Bhattacharya is one of the best professors I have had at GT. She is very accessible and helpful. She will respond to emails very quickly, and if your question can not be answered via email, she will set up a private call with you. As a non-ECE major, I felt she did a fantastic job at making the content understandable and not too complex.

One of the best professors at Georgia Tech hands down. Professor Bhattacharya is very caring and she really just wants you to understand the material. If you have any problems she will understand and help. The only downside is that the class was fully online this semester, but at least the lectures are recorded.

analysis.name: Nikki MacKenzie

Accounting is like learning a new language so it was extremely difficult at first, each test is worth a big chunk of your grade so be careful. She is a good professor with the hybrid class, and prepared us well for the exams. Don't fall behind, or else its extremely hard to catch back up and truly grasp the content as the tests are in depth

Accounting was new to me so I had to put a lot of time and effort into studying for this class. If you can avoid the hybrid class, do because I think in person would have been better.

idk if all the other reviews just were horrid test takers, but this was actually one of my lowest effort classes. read the syllabus, watch the lectures, do the practice in the notes, show up to class (or not honestly) and u will be fine. group project was annoying but manageable, plus done by thanksgiving. boring teacher but what can you do...

analysis.name: Nimisha Roy

Pretty great teacher. Was very receptive to feedback and genuinely wanted to improve the course. Lectures are pretty fast, but I found that to be a plus. Overall, 2340 as a class just sucks, but Prof. Roy tried her best to make it somewhat enjoyable.

agree with other reviewers in that ur experience rly depends on whether u get a good group or not. Prof. Roy did a great job with the hybrid format, although I am not a fan of the format.  She figured out a balance between hybrid, providing long-form videos with in-depth notes while summarizing the important details in class. Getting a good team in class really helps.

2340 is an awful class, but Professor Roy did a good job running it, and was very responsive to questions and overall helpful. The midterms and "mini-assessments" don't weigh a lot but require you to actually learn the "content", which led to poor performance that seemed to bother her more than it did the students. A fine professor for a bad class.

analysis.name: Namrata Dey Roy

Dr. Dey Roy was very caring and kind with students. The class was very fun and only had a few projects as grades and a final portfolio. What is needed to make an A is very clearly written in rubrics. We often watched movie clips and class discussion was a huge part of class. Occasional readings but no other homework besides that.

Professor essentially just read off textbook to us(Axler). There isn't much you'd get from lecture you couldn't get from reading the textbook.Grading felt unreasonable as I never recieved any feedback as to why I lost points.

His first time teaching the class. The content was quite hard and abstract, as was the homework. But after taking his course, I feel that I've learned how to self-study proofs and have improved my mathematical proof-writing.

Osvaldo is the man!! He is adorable, and I absolutely love this class. He is very smart and entertaining. If I could retake this class, I would. Osvaldo is amazing. Highly recommended.

He is a really cool guy and cares for his students. I didn't do as well as I would have liked but mainly because I didn't participate in class that much. He is difficult, but maybe just shouldn't be teaching 3111. I recommend him to people who are very knowledgeable of Spanish.
