Many exam questions were exact copies of homework questions, but no crib sheet or equation sheet. Letter grade was unknown until the end of the semester.

He attempts to keep us engaged in lectures. He shows derivations in lecture instead of applications, but HW and exams test on both. Weekly HW that's graded on accuracy, but textbook usually has answers. 80% of grade is from exams that reuse HW problems. He'll give more weight to your 2 midterms OR final, whichever you do better on.

Dr. Orlando is a great lecturer, but the class felt disorganized at times due to the nature of notes (no slides, only handwritten notes) and the grading scheme (he doesn't tell you what the A or B cut off is and there's random extra credit and participation points that aren't clear where they end up). Overall, good class but lots of effort needed.

The class/content was just hard itself- thermo/derivations. He posts hand-written lecture notes and goes over them in class; he'll tell you to read (I never did), but he clearly states what will be on his exams. Review sessions were helpful; study the homeworks. There were 2 exams + 1 final with 2 possible grading distributions. EC given.

Has handwritten notes that are really disorganized instead of any PowerPoints. During lectures, he goes on tangents. Tests are difficult but predictable. Extra credit involves reading scientific papers (most of which are his) and reviewing them. Class was fair enough, but it was disorganized, which made it a lot harder.

analysis.name: Dmitrii Ostrovskii
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: One of the best math profs I had at GT. Dmitrii is knowledgeable and clear with well-structured notes, plenty of textbook references, and intuitive and interesting examples. He will make you think and put real time in, but well worth it and makes one really learn. Highly recommend PhD students to take a class with him. Prereq in analysis is helpful

Dmitrii Ostrovskii is highly knowledgeable and supportive. He's very accessible outside class and gives great help in office hours, including useful research feedback. He also provides strong resources. The presentation/paper discussion (instead of an exam) was very helpful for grad studies.

You should take his class if you want to do math. I think the bad reviews come from people who expected high school level lectures or dislike math itself. He's very smart, I learned a lot in his class. Lectures were tough, yes —as a math class should— but grading's very soft. And his availability is without exaggeration the best I've had in GT.

Higher effort (and higher reward) than I anticipated. I think he understands what he teaches on a much much deeper level than teaching the class requires. Attendance isn't mandatory, but it's fun to see how passionate he is about the material. Definitely take if you care about getting an A (plenty of opportunities to do well) or you like research.

Prof. Ostrovskii is a fantastic teacher for a challenging subject. He presents high-dimensional statistics with clarity and insight, connects theory to intuition, and is very thoughtful in his explanations.

analysis.name: Olivier Pierron
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 34 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Great teacher. Definitely knows his stuff. Entertaining lecturer and lectures are very important. Tests make up 80% of grade and they are never curved so be prepared on test days. Overall very reasonable and makes the class enjoyable.

Pierron was not good for dynamics. It was his 2nd semester teaching it, 90% of grade is from exams (2 midterms, 1 final) and 10% is from HW. HW is just write 2 questions about the problem. Spent too much time deriving equations, attempted examples, didn't finish them and sometimes was wrong. Class attendance dropped so much after a few weeks.

Dr. Pierron is a great professor. While the class itself can present difficult problems, Dr. Pierron's lecture style involves working through a variety of different scenarios using a game plan that can be applied to any statics problem! 90% of your grade is from tests, but he's a fair grader and you know what to expect each time. Caring guy too!

Avoid if bad at physics, otherwise you'll be fine. 2 midterms + 1 final = 90% of grade (9 problems total). Takes off a lot of points for messing up the math, but also gives 70% for just showing you know the process. 
Depends how you look at it, for me it made the tests more stressful and that didn't go well with my other classes.

His lectures were clear and well structured, and the homework was a breeze. However, 90% of the grade was from the exams (40% midterms and 50% final exam), so you absolutely must do well on them. Fortunately, all of the problems are from the textbook, so pracitce well and you should be fine.

analysis.name: Lee Oh
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 30 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: There are so many things I want to say but not enough words in the English language to describe how goated she is. All I will say is she has never given a student below an A in her entire history of this course and she gave out max score on every assignment as long as you did it. Goated person and goated course.

I took KOR3415 online with her. The best professor ive had at tech, she is so sweet and genuinely cares about her students. she is very understanding, and her feedback on assignments is so kind. best prof ever

I can not even begin to describe how amazing of a teacher Mrs. Oh is. She is caring, kind, funny, and a great teacher. She always has a smile and never makes anyone feel left out of the class. She wants you to succeed and asks how you are doing even outside of class. 100/10 teacher and would take again if I could!

Professor Oh was incredibly kind, funny, and helpful. She made the class fun with plenty of games and snacks. We moved pretty quickly in lecture but homework was graded easily, so it was easy to make an A in the class if you studied. There was less emphasis on speaking and practice, but she provided office hours for those looking to talk.
One of the best professors at GT! She is quite caring and provides great feedback on assignments. This was an overall easy class but engaging.

analysis.name: Phanish Suryanarayana
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: His lectures are entirely from the textbook, and the examples he does in class are the ones already solved in the book. Don't really need to go to class. Homework is straight out of the book. Tests are not too bad if you understand the examples and homework problems. Final exam was much harder than any test. WARNING: he grades on a bell curve.

Prof. Suryanaryana is a really solid professor. it's only my first sem. at Tech and considering he's my only real "engineering" class take this with a grain of salt. As other's have said, he goes off of textbooks, but doesn't post his lectures online: VERY lenient grader on tests, and utilize TA for hw's cuz its free 25%, do textbook problems

First test wasn't too bad, but the class kind of bombed it, so he made the second test and final so much easier with extra credit. He gave out so much extra credit, so as long as you go to class and somewhat understand physics 1, you'll probably be fine.
His lectures are very boring, and he doesnt upload any content whatsoever onto canvas so it is very hard to continue learning outside of the classroom

Skip lectures if you want. Just study textbook or notes you will be fine. Grading will be very easy. Professor will make the final easy if he sees everyone struggling in the class. HW are directly from TB.

analysis.name: Milos Prvulovic
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very caring and passionate professor, who is interested in answering students' questions and helping them out. Good guy, interesting lectures. Easy test(s).The projects were a logistical mess though -- released quite late into the semester, deadlines adjusted, but still we had less time and test cases didn't seem well written, causing stress for me

All the time spent for this class is on the 5 projects. You have the test cases, so pretty much a guaranteed 100 if you spend enough time and finish. 1 midterm that is pretty much IDENTICAL to the practice exam lol. Made our final optional; can use our midterm as final exam grade. Didn't go to lecture because repeat of 2200/4290. Relaxing class

Wonderful professor, makes the class very engaging and answers all in-class questions with good enthusiasm. Exploratory teaching. Slides content might be little poor but he makes up for it with good lectures

Generally good professor. Exam was super easy (exact same as the practice he gave out). Projects, although usually easy, are plagued with issues in the instructions. In many projects, students had to post their own workarounds for the rest of class to rely on. TA announcements often contradict the assignment instructions, which don't seem to update

Milos is very knowledgeable regarding what he teaches. This course was honestly really easy this semester. The lectures were interesting for the first 2/3 of the course, but then he ran out of content and they fell off. The projects weren't too hard, you could have a partner. The instructions were wrong sometimes too.

analysis.name: Raghuram Pucha
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 93 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor was boring overall. Lectures were fine, work was easy, he posted all the instructions to canvas and you could figure out most of the coursework without his help. Very available in office hours. The problem came when we were presenting our final projects. He insulted groups projects in front of the whole class and was incredibly unkind.

Boring. Switch if you can for this course. All was fine in the class until the project presentation where no real rubric was given and then he was upset that projects did not have what he wish they would. After a section of presentations, he complained that he was tired and called other groups boring and said he was falling asleep watching them.

So much work. The drawing portion of class (first 4 week) had so much HW. After that, all homework and lab assignments could be completed in class. There is an individual and group project that occur concurrently, which take quite a bit of time to do. Its a lot of work but easy A. TAs knew nothing. He is impossible to reach via email - go office hr

I heard horror stories about him from upperclassmen before I took his class, but he was far and away my favorite prof. and class in Fall 2025. I entirely learned SolidWorks from no prior experience just due to his teaching and assignments. If you're looking for easier, try Jacobsen or Dorozhkin, but if you want to really learn, take Pucha.

Despite being a 3 credit hour class it felt like a 5 credit hour class. You will do much more work outside of class compared to other ME 1670 professors. There are also often unclear instructions on assignments. Very difficult to access outside of office hours as well, responded to 1 of 4 emails sent throughout the semester.

analysis.name: Matthew Powell
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I would give zero stars if I could. Professor docks points for not showing work in the exact format he has in his head. Students shouldn't be punished for actually understanding the material. If the answer is right, the thinking was right. Avoid if you have any other option. Your grade will reflect his inane standards, not your actual knowledge.

Don't be fooled by the bad reviews. Powell is a very good teacher, multi is just a class that combines linear algebra and calculus so people dont do well. His lectures are very clear and paced well, and you are encouraged to ask questions or solve examples in class. I would definitely reccommend taking his class.

Best professor I had this semester. Very passionate about abstract linear algebra. Exams and quizzes are manageable if you are willing to work. The class was tough due to many abstract concepts but it was fun and worth it. Definitely choose over MATH 1554, but expect to work harder.

Although professor doesn't really matter for large classes, I will say this professor teaches like you're reading a mathematics proof textbook. He introduces topics in a very formal and theorem based way followed by examples I found helpful. It works for some ppl and doesn't for others. He's a knowledgeable guy, but remember it more abt the TAs.

Dr. Powell is one of the greatest professors I've encountered- not only are his lectures phenomenal, they're accessible for people of all ability levels, and he is very approachable for question and clarification- including mid lecture.

analysis.name: Hang (Jerry) Qi
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He was super nice, but not the best lecturer. A lot of the example problems were never finished and he does have a bit of an accent which made it hard to understand him. Exams were exactly like the homeworks and you got 3 attempts per question. He was also very generous with partial credit. If you study the practice problems, you should do good.

Great professor. Def bods can be a little rough sometimes, but he is good at explaining the concepts and will go over them again if you ask questions. Would take again 10/10. The homework is from the most recent ed. of the textbook, but I just checked it out of the library when I needed it, and I relied on his lectures and slides for the material.

He's a decent, not great lecturer but has a really good understanding of the material and gives good advice. Tests and homework assignments are very short with multiple attempts per question and generous extra credit. The tests are very heavily weighted and the final is 40% though.

easy to follow if you pay attention in class. very similar to physics; if you understand physics this class will be fine. the only thing is that the class is very test heavy (exam 1 15%, exam 2 25%, and final 40%), but the homework helps set up well for the exam problems

Bad lectures, hard to understand. Left practice problems unfinished. Gave good partial credit on hw and exams. 3 attempts per question on hw and exams. Exam questions came directly from textbook, which had over 100 per chapter. Self study class basically

analysis.name: Qirun Zhang
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very kind and approachable professor who is always willing to help and accessible during office hours. However, in-class examples were often hard to see due to small writing. Piazza responses were sometimes indirect, and grades were released very late, making it hard to know how I was doing before the drop deadline.

Really nice person, very clearly cares about students. Would even go out of his way to talk to me outside of class. That being said, he's a pretty dry lecturer and he teaches off slides he did not make. He does not answer most piazza questions with anything other than 'talk to me after class or in OH'. However, homeworks and projects are easy.

Professor Zhang is a very good lecturer who describes the content in a very methodical manner. The projects, while really involved, were super interesting, and I feel like I understand a part of Computer Science that was opaque to me before this class.

analysis.name: Moinuddin Qureshi
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Took ECE6100 (x-listed with ECE4100 & CS4290/6290). Lectures are not recorded so attendance is mandatory if you want to do well on exams, though, they are very engaging. Exams are a little tricky but there are ungraded "homeworks" with very similar questions and you can make a crib sheet. Labs are fine if you know C/C++ and are fairly scaffolded.

Awesome lecturer but slides aren't so good and lectures are not recorded. So attendance is mandatory if you actually want to understand content. Also made the first lab worth 4% of final grade due during the first week (while registration is still open). Exams are difficult purely bc of the amount of content you need to know, there is a lot

Great professor! Probably the best lecturer I've had at GT. Teaches with slides but in the most ideal way possible by using them as support, not as a crutch. Projects were fairly simple if you have decent C/C++ knowledge. Tests were a little difficult as they were modeled after the ungraded homeworks, but doable.

Lectures like he's telling a story–makes potentially dry content exciting. Projects were excellent (if a bit hand-holdy), with smooth debugging and problems interesting enough to ponder AFK. Tests felt hard, but there was so much curving and extra credit by the end that lower quartile class grade was a comfy A. Kind prof who will brighten your day!

Took CS6290 (crosslisted 4290/ECE 4100/6100). Lectures extremely engaging and helpful. Tests a bit different than lecture content, but studying previous exams and practicing computations helped. Final was ‘Do-No-Harm'. Projects were well structured with TA debug outs. Small extra credit opportunities were given. Favorite prof at GT, he knows a lot!

analysis.name: Qi Wang
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She is super passionate about film and also extremely educated. The lectures can be boring since she doesn't follow a specific curriculum, but cool. The entire grade is based off attendance, your final, and weekly film screenings. She def gets strict when it comes to the final project and your essay/film ideas. Do NOTTT take it at 5pm

Very sweet and passionate about film. Lecture isn't always the most engaging since she's speaking the whole time. For final projects, there were creative options (short films, poster series) or you could write a 10 page paper. She's always open to questions and is very willing to help or give feedback.

I think Dr. Wang is the victim of a poorly executed curriculum. She is a wonderful and bubbly human being, but she failed to pique my interest in film. Her class is strictly attendance mandatory (25% of grade) but absolutely does NOT need to be at all. She's worth taking as a professor in general, but I would avoid this course specifically.

Professor Wang is sweet and caring. But most of the grade goes to the midterm and final essays, which must be 10+ pages long, personally, I found that a bit annoying. There's a screening and a discussion about it on Canvas every week but you only have to finish 7 discussions for the semester.

Professor Wang is very sweet and cares a lot about her students. Lectures are long but the topics are often interesting and it isn't vital that you pay attention or read the textbook. The only grades are attendance, a weekly discussion post on an assigned movie, and two papers. She is very lenient on extensions for the papers and easy to contact.

analysis.name: Dong Qin
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Qin puts in a lot of effort to be accessible and helpful for students. She cares that students learn the material and tries to keep interest high in the content. Tests are relatively straightforward if you read the textbook and lecture notes, but grading is very strict which can make getting high grades difficult.

As person, I liked Dr. Qin. She loves talking to students and having them show up to office hours. However, her lectures can be confusing and she doesnt explain concepts well. The quizzes are alright, and exams are pretty easy.
Quiz every week so be prepared and ask questions often. Not the easiest MSE2001 class, but I learned a decent bit, just make sure you know the material in-depth and do the optional hw.

Dr. Qin is SO caring and gives great lectures. However, she's a very tough grader, and oftentimes expectations for assignments weren't super clear because of that. The expectations are high and can be tough to meet. Overall, I learned a lot from her lectures and assignments but this class will be more effort than you expect if you are an undergrad.

Qin is great, nice, & helpful. KEY TO AN A: During her lectures and office hours, if you listen to her, you'll hear tons of hints as to what will be on the test and by the time it shows up, you'll basically know what exactly is on it. The tests and quizes are also basically the exact same every semester so just study them & you'll do great.

analysis.name: Qiqin Xie
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The professor's grading structure has changed from previous years; there is no EC on quizzes and only a little on exams. The content and lectures are mind-numbingly boring. Homework is not graded so the exams are weighted heavily. I went to her office hours twice, once she was late the other time she did not show up.

Lectures aren't the most exciting but cover everything on the tests. Helpful but infrequent office hours. Generous class policies/extra credit and the tests are reasonable but not easy by any means. Can be hard to follow the lectures because they move so fast and cover so many topics, but that's just combo for you. Would recommend her in general.

She gives a HUGE amount of extra credit that it's almost impossible not to make an A. She is a great lecturer and explains concepts clearly, and she's very good at explaining what you're expected to know for exams/quizzes. She gives 20 pts extra credit on quizzes and 100 pts (yes, 100 pts) extra credit on tests. You can exempt the final (95+ avg)

Theres no extra credit on quizzes, only 20-30 points on tests (but its usually EXTREMELY hard to earn even one of these). Quizzes are weekly and difficult, and theres only two tests and a final besides that. She does give other extra credit opportunities that I would recommend taking advantage of. Its a hard class but I heard she's the easiest.

Weekly quizzes are time-consuming to prep for but not bad if you review lecture notes. The textbook isn't bad for this course but I mainly prepped for assessments using her lecture notes. For her exams, the best way to study for them is to go through the practice questions released before each exam (problems mimic those practice questions).

analysis.name: Richard Vuduc
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: God awful class. Teach yourself to code, no instruction or guidance, fill out the notebooks to perfection after 20 hours of banging your head against your keyboard, hope TA is helpful, over rely on AI cause whats even happening, take exams with unforgiving autograder, heres some math sprinkled in here and there. whole course needs to be redone

The pace of the course is quite quick. All the work is through Vocareum(similar to Jupiter), but the app has issues with connection and others. Vocareum may grade you wrong because it only recognizes commands given during class.The class is online and there's little support from instructors.The class needs a lot more instructors to help students
The lectures are completely unrelated to the exams and the professor clearly doesn't care about teaching or whether students actually understand the material.
Teach what you don't test. Test what you don't teach. That is his motto.
I think professor Vuduc gave up on teaching a long time ago. Google could teach this class better. Tests do not reflect anything actually learned. Do yourself a favor and prepare for the worst

analysis.name: Tobias Ried
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I am pretty sure this is his first class, so he didn't really have a great grasp of what type of exam to give. He sounds like a nice guy in class, but the first exam was diabolical. There are a lot of improvements he can and should make as a professor overall, but outside of that exam, I cant really hate him. Pray in his next class he improves.

I will say this. Professor Reid is a very kind and caring man, and he genuinely does seem like he wants success for his class. I emailed him to speak a bit louder and a tiny bit slower during lectures, and the very next day he made the improvement. That said, he operates using the European style of teaching, which can be rough if you're like me.

Super nice person and professor. He very passionate about what he teaches and you can tell. However, if all differential classes were theory heavy I wouldn't have a problem, but comparing test to different sections it's kind of ridiculous. If you like theory take it with him, if you want an easier A just take it with someone else.

Professor Ried is a great professor. The lectures and tests are theory focused, yet he cares about what he teaches. He wants his students to succeed. When he gave a hard exam, he clarified he did not intend any harm, and gave a great curve for the whole class. He is very caring and is energetic during lectures every day.

analysis.name: Robbie Richards
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: richards is a very approachable man, and is also very clearly passionate about what he teaches which makes the class more engaging. however, sometimes he would struggle with finishing the class on time because the lectures were so slide heavy and have so much content to go through. very approachable guy and also clearly cares about the students.

Dr. Richards co-taught 1108 with Dr. Birol. Richards focused more on the plant and fungus concepts. Although my area of focus is animals, I preferred Dr. Richards' lectures. He is a passionate speaker and clearly loves biology. I went to only 70% of the lectures (mostly when Richards was teaching) and got a comfortable A in this class.

He is very passionate about biology and does a good job explaining the material. The exams are not too difficult as long as you do the practice problems and go through the lectures. There is some work outside of class to prepare for each lecture but it does help you understand.

One of my favorite professors at gatech so far! His class offers a very clear, very beginner-friendly introduction to R. The lectures are very informative and while you technically do not have to attend them, the slides themselves can be dense and he walks through them very clearly in lecture so I would recommend attending class.

I had Dr. Richards for BIOS 1108. While I liked his personality, the class relied on Learning Catalytics, which I found unhelpful. His lectures often missed key content in the learning objectives, leaving me to teach myself after class. The learning objectives were the most helpful part, guiding my focus on relevant material outside of class.

analysis.name: Sandra Reid
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This might just be me, but I personally had a hard time understanding Professor Reid's lectures. I'd already taken this class in high school, so I didn't learn anything from going to her lectures. However, whenever I did go to them, they explained simple problems in a complicated manner. The tests were always way harder than the lectures as well.

Professor Ried is hands down one of the best Math 1551 professors out there. Her lectures are very entertaining and she always uploads her lectures with additional help if needed. Besides her lectures, she is very helpful in Office Hours. If you at least pay attention in class and went to Office Hours, Math 1551 is a breeze. She really is the GOAT!

I feel like exams are graded weirdly and do not accurately show your knowledge or progress. There is nothing that I specifically disliked about her, I just don't think she explained the material too deeply or explicitly.

Very chill professor. I went to about every lecture and at first she didn't use the screen and did everything on white board, but then mid semester she genuinely took the feedback from the surveys and switched to doing it with the screen. She's obviously new at teaching, but for a first ever semester teaching, she did a better job than most profs.
It was obvious that she was new to teaching, however it seemed as if she really cared. Hopefully with time the teaching becomes a lot better and easier to understand.

analysis.name: Sedina Tsikata
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Tsikata is very caring and helpful. For fluid mechanics, whilst attendance isn't mandatory, you will really be screwed if you miss a lecture! Also, the lectures themselves are quite long and have SO MUCH material, so I personally found it difficult to digest all of the information and feel comfortable for the exams.

Homework 40%, 2 Midterms 15% each, Final 30%. Lectures were boring, and slides had a lot of information, but not all of it was necessary. Grading took forever. Concepts were difficult, but she was nice and cared. A seemed impossible after M2, but there was a lot of extra credit at the end to get points back on the midterm and a curve for the final.

Short Answer: Only take if you don't want to learn the subject or want an easy class. DO NOT TAKE HER IF YOU WANT TO LEARN.

Dr. Tsikata is by far my least favorite professor I have had at GT. She does not care about you or teaching. She showed up late to class routinely and constantly had excuses for severely delayed grading and posting solutions.

Dr. Tsikata is a great human being. Her lecture slides can be somewhat uninspired, but she deeply cares about her students. I learned a great deal in her class. She is very busy, so she may seem distant at times due to this. I would recommend her still.

Often unreachable and condescending. She's straight up said she wouldn't respond to emails after I told her she had missed mine for a couple weeks. The one midterm we had was overtly difficult and no one finished in the allotted time. The content wasn't bad but the professor made it worse than it had to be

analysis.name: Sashank Varma
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: take robotics instead if youre a cs major

Attendance is required one lecture a week as he gives quizzes based on readings (they're either through Canvas with a code or paper and pencil), and these quizzes are very specific so you have to actually read. The rest of the class is very chill (though it depends on the TA) and easy to do well in. Plus the content is actually pretty interesting!
lots of reading and essay writing, course is structured more like a humanities course than a CS course. take robotics instead.

He really knows his material. I only participated in about half the classes (i.e. the days we had quizzes), but even then I could tell just how good of a lecturer he is. Looking back, I do regret not attending and paying attention to every single lecture, because I think I would've gotten a ton out of this class. Weekly discussion posts and papers.

Class is overall pretty easy. LOTS OF READING!! I only went to class the day we would have quizzes bc his lecture style wasn't beneficial to me but I enjoyed the structure of the class. ACTUALLY READ THE READINGS because he will ask the most specific questions on weekly quizzes that you wont know unless you read it (even though they are open note)

analysis.name: Zhaiming Shen
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Bad lecturer- mumbles and is quiet, only writes on whiteboard and stands in front of what he's writing. Gives less resources than others (seems that he took stuff off the syllabus when comparing with another section). Posts lecture slides, but unfinished, and we never get through them in lecture anyways, so I just watch Lehmann's videos.

He only writes on the small board at the front of the class even though there are four projectors for him to show his work, making lecture hard to follow. His tests are way harder than the slides and quizzes, and he often posts lecture slides with no solutions or methodology. He also has a big accent. First test average was 62/100.

Do not take.

Spring 2025. was very difficult for me even though I took it in high school. I'm not sure if I struggled so much due to poor teaching or my own stupidity. I thought he was a fine lecturer but obviously I didn't do so well in the class. He used the whiteboards instead of a projector, and it was really hard for me to see with the glare and distance.

Writes on a small whiteboard in the front of a classroom with 4 projectors (he doesn't use any of them, so it's difficult to follow his work), bad accent, makes tests impossible and unfair (first two tests had an average of 66/100, and the last one had a 61/100 average)

He doesn't run the class well and I would not recommend him.
Very Tough on the grading. Tests much harder than other 2551 sections

analysis.name: Tianchi Yang
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very difficult to understand and handwriting during class is tough to decipher. Took the class based on reviews before mine but he must have changed things because the exams were nothing like the practice or lecture and ridiculously difficult. When approached about why exams were so difficult he mumbled something about "figuring it out". AVOID HIM!

Administered this class incredibly well by himself (no TA). Publishes handwritten and typeset notes for each lecture. HW/exam solutions are posted promptly (even before grades are returned), and grading is generous. Clear topic lists and past exams with solutions make exams very approachable. Lectures are textbook-definition-oriented, which I like.

Just got done taking the hardest midterm of my life. Talked to friends in other sections whose exams where SIGNIFICANTLY easier. Has an atrocious accent. Only good thing about him is he responds to Ed posts pretty quickly

Well-organized professor, communicates class information and requirements clearly. Has written and typeset notes available after each lecture. Exams don't feel like overloaded mistake minefields, and past exams make them easy to study for.
Worst professor I've ever taken; his exams are no where near the difficult of the practice exams!

analysis.name: Thad Starner
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 96 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I respect Professor Starner for his research and intelligence; however, as an instructor, Thad is woefully inadequate. He gives the bare minimum in terms of content, the rest is self-taught. This class is the grad class, even after promises of change. Due to the poor structure of this class, I will walk out of this class learning nothing.

Thad, what a guy. What to say, so many things. Homeworks are extremely hard. For lectures, only go if you're interested in knowing Thad's career, and how cool his Google glasses are! Make sure if you do take this class, Dr. Mark Riedl is teaching. Thad should go back to industry jobs because his idea of teaching is disgusting. He makes me sick.

This class is not an undergrad class, so you are expected to do graduate-level work. Most grad students are taking 1 or 2 classes so they can balance the assignments while I'm having to neglect other classes. The prof doesn't do anything to acknowledge the unfairness of this and just mentions how this is applicable to Google interviews.

Any spark of interest I had in AI was almost immediately snuffed out within the few weeks of Thad's class. This was the most demotivating and unenjoyable experience I've ever had with learning at Tech. The best thing about this class is that it made me incredibly grateful for my other classes, TAs, and professors this semester.

Horrible professor. He got awful grading for midterm exams and hilarious regrade requests. If you would like to suffer, I would suggest you take Thad's class. It is endless torture. Also, his grading is so funny that even if you get a score above 90 for an exam/assignment, you still might get a B for that exam/assignment.

analysis.name: David Torello
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 38 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: If you expect your students to attend class and be on time, the professor should hold himself to those standards. He was late almost every class and took about 20 minutes each class (not exaggerating) to set up his ipad and talk about off topic things. I got an A in physics 2, but had to withdraw from this class.  Please be on time to teach.

One of the best profs I've ever had at Tech. Dynamics is hard, but he's put A LOT of work into giving you all the resources you need to succeed. The class is very organized imo; he has loads of review problems, class notes, and office hours are always helpful (he gives free snacks 😎). He's lenient on due dates and super understanding.

Graded on 5 projects, first 2 were with a partner which made it a lot less overwhelming. Office hours were a great resource for anything relating to the project. Every single project got extended which was nice, but good luck trying to contact him outside of class. Super nice guy, lectures were just inherently boring but he tried his best

Probably the best professor I have ever had!  He is really nice and makes classes pretty entertaining. His grading criteria is this pass or fail thing which is honestly pretty forgiving but damn it stressed me out. Like every exam question is essentially a letter grade in weight but he lets you redo one per test.  Super hard to reach very busy guy.

Went in fearing Dynamics and came out grudgingly admitting I liked it. He's THAT prof: has clear lectures breaking down intimidating material, has infectious enthusiasm, builds community, has unhinged idgaf humor, deeply cares about each student. Dynamics is HARD, but his grading criteria makes it very possible to do well & is oddly forgiving.

analysis.name: Thomas Tran
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 30 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Lectures are heavy but participation is not required. VERY webworks heavy. Studio worksheets / class are very confusing and fast paced so give yourself grace. He offers extra credit for attending office hours towards the end of the semester / completing all your webworks. Very understanding and wants students to succeed. He came through for me.

Great lecturer, really broke down problems in an easy to understand way. Homework was just weekly Webwork, and exams were somewhat difficult but he drops your lowest midterm which is helpful. Final grade is about 80 percent exams, but he does curve the final grades. Great professor!

He seems kind and makes time esp during exam week but I had a hard time following his lectures and often found myself struggling to stay awake. Has def been annoying towards the end of the sem and been forceful about students talking to each other to the point of coming up to students individually, which is frustrating as someone w/ bad anxiety...

I absolutely loved having him as my professor. He was so caring and gave some opporutnities for grade improvement. This is a professor that wants students to do well in his class. With that being said, his exams are HARD. He gives lots of practice problems but the test is always hard. Also be prepared to do a lot of Webworks.

Great at teaching and explaining the content, and you can tell that he cares about his students! He always reminded us about any upcoming tests or quizzes, and he always made sure we were aware of what content they would be over. Practice exams are a big help for test prep. Highly recommend taking his class if you get the chance!

analysis.name: Lily Turaski
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 37 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Turaski is simply wonderful. She wants you to succeed in her class, provides you with all the tools and extra credit to do it, but more importantly is just an amazing human being. She helped me (a disabled student) with lots of things regarding my accommodations and genuinely cared about my wellbeing. Take her if at all possible!

Take her class if you can! She is an incredible lecturer who makes it easy to understand the content. Unlike other MSE 2001 professors, she offers the chance to correct missed test questions and get some points back, and she also had 3 optional mini-projects for extra credit. She wants you to succeed, and she gives you all the tools to do it! 12/10

Turaski is an amazing professor. Her lectures are extremely engaging and she even sometimes does demos during class. She even makes sure to connect MSE topics with other areas of engineering, which is pretty nice. Weekly HW is easy and there's fun mini-projects for extra credit. Highly recommend!

Best teacher for MSE2001 by far! The content for MSE2001 can be boring at times, but Turaski is always engaging and makes the class fun by doing mini demos of certain topics. In addition, she was the only teacher to give extra credit on exams by doing test corrections. In addition, there are optional mini-projects that act as extra credit too!

She's young and enthusiastic. You can tell she loves MSE and cares about her students. I enjoyed her class. It definitely reminded me of high school, being taught off of slides, memorization-heavy, and multiple choice homework/tests.

analysis.name: Umakishore Ramachandran
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 76 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I genuinely don't understand all these horrible reviews. Ramachandran clearly knows his stuff, and it shows. Most people's issue seems to be that the class is tough. I can't stand when bum students blame professors instead of putting in the work to master hard concepts. There are countless resources to help you succeed.

I'm sure Kishore means well - but that's truly not enough for a course with such heavy and important content. Lectures are meaningless as Kishore tends to come unprepared and start lecture late, go on unrelated ramblings speak extremely fast and annotate powerpoint slides in such a fashion that no one can understand what is supposed to be learned.

He is a very nice guy, and super understanding and helpful one on one. But his class is really hard and he goes through his powerpoint slides (which haven't changed since the 90s) really quickly. He doesn't understand questions in class, and always has mandatory clicker questions. TAs for this class are useless. So good luck to you.

The concepts covered in this course are tremendously important for software engineers. Unfortunately, it's taught horrendously. The course needs to be entirely revamped, and steer far clear from the rote learning and mindless calculation path that it's currently on.

Kishore is not a great lecturer. slides are messy, often rambles is confusing, and just uses point solutions to force students to come. Most people watch Daglis' lectures instead, which are far more coherent. 2200 is probably the hardest class I've taken at Tech, exams are difficult and they grade Ed participation to make up for no TA involvement.

analysis.name: Aselia Urmanbetova
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 58 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: She is a great professor who cares about her students grades, and well-being, while it being an online class. She did go MIA for about a month or two which led to many students paniking, but she gave us all extra credit to make up for it and the class itself (Macro) was very interesting, and enjoyable which made it not too difficult.

Dr.U is amazing. She is such a caring and kind professor, even to students she has never met face-to-face. You have so many opportunities to do well in this class. The canvas is super well structured, instructions for assignments are very clear, and overall it is a well-organised class. Do well on HW and practice tests, and you'll ace the exams.

Her class is structured for her students to succeed. She gives a quiz every week on the material covered during the week, which sounds awful, but actually allows you to study in increments and you learn the material a lot better. There are 14 quizzes and 0 tests, so if you mess up badly on one quiz, its not a big deal. Would recommend.
A great and caring professor. Responds quickly and has options for extra credit, and is always easy to work with. She has a groupme where she interacts with students and answers any questions.

Easy A, but you have to actively check Canvas for any changes because the dates change a lot. I also felt that she was not as easy to talk to as she made herself seem, since she hardly ever replied to texts. She was in our GroupMe and there were about 5 questions per day, but she chose one day like 2 days before the exam to reply to them all.

analysis.name: Richard Utz
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Utz is a very passionate professor, and I would recommend him to everyone. He truly cares about his students and wants them to succeed. He does have pop quizzes based on the books; however, they are very basic, and if you have read the text, you should do well on them. He is a bit eccentric (in a good way) and very encouraging!!
W prof. I was concerned that I wouldn't get an A by the end of the semester and I really shouldn't have mathematically, but I still did.

While Dr. Utz can be a bit of a handful sometimes, if you can meet him at his level, he is an incredibly intelligent, witty, and kind professor. He is a bit eccentric (which I like) but knows what he's talking about. He clearly cares about student success in and out of the classroom. Worth taking in my opinion, but not for everyone.

Holy respect on this guy. He used to be Dean of Ivan Allen and is now the assistant dean, so he has a ton of knowledge about LMC. This class is an intro to LMC class, so we get a taste of the faculty under LMC. He's lenient when it comes to grading as long as you follow ALL his instructions. Very engaging with the class, also has fun stories

TAKE THIS PROFESSOR!! He's such an easy grader. Wants everyone to succeed and is always available outside of class. You'll have an assignment due about once a month. Two solo and one group presentation. No homework, test, or quizzes. Extra credit if you activity participate. Class is "mandatory" but wont dock your grade if you don't show up.

analysis.name: Jun Ueda
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He is a great professor, young and friendly. Never shows arrogance nor does torment you with abstract math. Although I sometimes couldn't understand his lectures due to his accent I go back and watched lectures again, which made so much sense. Prepares you well for the quizzes and exams. Expect 4 quizzes, a midterm and a final that aren't too hard.
Good Professor, many exams but learnt a lot at the end of the course.

Lectures are a bit slow, but he will tell you everything you need to know. Homework's are long, but well chosen and good practice. Two exams are challenging, but fair, and there are many similar practice problems available. Often extra credit problems on the exams. Matlab computer project was interesting and doable. Final can be exempted.

Lectures are honestly not the best but his class structure allows you to easily get an A. Midterms are very similar to practice so as long as you study that you'll be fine. Final can be exempted. Extra credit questions were on the midterms. There is a final project but if you do good on the midterms you don't need to put a lot of effort.

Dr. Ueda cares a lot about his students, and his class is easy relative to the subject matter. Most people stopped going to class, but he still takes time to send many announcements and give relevant resources. HW tends to be pretty lengthy, but the tests are very similar to resources given. If you put effort in, he will definitely notice!

analysis.name: Usha Nair-Reichert
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Two tests made up 72% of the grade, and the final could replace one. She frequently cancelled class for various legitimate reasons. Her online replacement classes made it a chore to learn the relatively easy textbook material needed for the exams. She clearly cares and tries to engage, but the 75-minute classes felt very long.

There is no room for making mistakes in this class. After missing an assignment deadline, I was asked to see her and was completely humiliated during the meeting. The teaching style is disorganized and not engaging at all. She is completely inconsiderate of other people's time and personal circumstances.

Long boring lectures, so a 75 minute lecture felt like 5 hours. Was late to every single class by 10 mins. Three tests, (one dropped), last two were one week apart which was awful.The homeworks were not planned well. Claims to be approachable but when I emailed + went to office hours it just seemed like she wanted to be done as quick as possible.

Professor UNR is very kind and cares for her students. It is easy to do well in her class as her grading scheme is helpful. The in-class assignments are randomly spread but are quite easy, as are quizzes and tests. Reading the definition sections of the textbook was enough for exams as they are basic applications of concepts.

Prof.Nair was one of the best professors I've ever had. Great lectures. She is the most caring (genuinely asks about mental health, open to discuss it too). She did her best to give everyone in our class an A (extra credit, optional tests to replace bad grades...). She also knows the answer to ANYTHING you could ask. Love her.

analysis.name: Jerry Ulrich
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I had Dr. U as part of the Oxford Study Abroad Program, and he was great! Most of our classes were group presentations to make it more interesting than him just lecturing to us for 2 hours. Each group had 2 presentations, and they made up a big part of our grade. He tells you exactly what to expect on the tests and makes sure you're prepared.

Best guy in the world!! I had never sang before, but he is so motivating! I came to this class just to have fun, Dr. U cracks jokes the whole time. He encourages an awesome sense of community. Basically, practices are twice a week and then a final performance..if you go you get an A. this teacher is one of the most loving persons I have ever met
I really didn't learn much, but it was easy I guess.

I LOVE Dr. U!!! He changed my life. I had never sang before and was very nervous. He is just such a great person to be around, he really understands people in general and he loves them too. He is hilarious and the class is just a good time.
Doc is great! He's really funny, and Glee was the best "class" I've ever had. Plus, you get graded on showing up and trying to sing. If you do that, you get an A.

analysis.name: Jan Uelzmann
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Absolutely wonderful man. Clearly loves teaching and is very engaging. Loves his wife and kids, and 90s German music. Has great sense of fashion, sporting tasteful Pink Floyd and black shirts, not to mention V-necks. Classy dude all in all.

I loved him and enjoyed the class. He's very nice and clearly very knowledgable and passionate about the topic. Class wasn't super difficult, just a decent amount of reading/listening. There's a few papers and a project among other things, but it's not super time-consuming.

Uelzmann is fantastic. He's really passionate about post war literature and lectures were so engaging. He does tend to get really enthusiastic, which often leads to him speaking moderately quickly in German, just be aware of that. The whole class is taught in German, but he's super nice about explaining words you might not know. Highly recommend!

Dr. Uelzmann is easily the best german teacher I have had. I had been taking german for 6 years when I started 2001. I took that to get an easy A. However, Dr. Uelzmann challenged me and turned me into a better student. He also was very sympathetic to the fact that I had mono. He really cares for his students and his class content. Take Uelzmann!

I highly recommend taking Jan when you can. Seriously, he is one of the funniest, enthusiastic and approachable teacher I have seen. He speaks mostly in German, but adds actions to make you understand what he is saying. Also as a final example, he laughs with the class about the world wars whereas all my previous teachers took offense!
